bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues give me the highest possible utility: issueA=valueF (1.0), issueB=valueF (1.0), issueC=valueF (1.0), issueD=valueF (1.0). This is the natural opening position — anchor high and concede gradually as time progresses and I learn the opponent's preferences.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me very low utility (0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 + 0.3*0.20 = 0.20). We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay close to my top utility bid. My opening was all valueF (utility = 1.0). I'll make a very small concession on issueA (my least important issue at 0.1 weight) by dropping to valueE, while holding firm on the more important issues.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all valueA (minimum utility for me) and hasn't moved at all after two rounds. It's very early (t=0.04), so I have no pressure to concede significantly. I'll stay near my top utility while making a small concession on issueA (low weight for me) to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll hold firm on issueC and issueD (highest weights) and make a minor concession on issueB.

The opponent is consistently offering their best bid (all valueA) which gives me very low utility (0.2 across all issues = ~0.2 total). They haven't conceded at all. It's very early (t=0.06), so I should stay firm near my top utility while making small concessions. I'll concede slightly on issueA (low weight 0.1) and issueB (weight 0.2) while holding firm on issueC and issueD (both weight 0.3). I'll try issueB: valueD to vary from my previous bid.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their maximum-utility bid (all valueA) every single turn, showing no concession whatsoever. It's very early (t=0.08), so I should not rush to concede significantly. I'll continue making small concessions on low-importance issues (issueA, issueB) while holding firm on my high-importance issues (issueC, issueD). I'll try a slightly different combination to avoid repeating my last bid exactly.
